questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 本公司的應用程式使用部署在 Amazon VPC 中的網路負載平衡器、Auto Scaling 群組、Amazon EC2 執行個體和資料庫。該公司希望在其 Amazon VPC 中近乎即時地捕獲進出網路介面的流量資訊。該公司希望將資訊傳送到 Amazon OpenSearch Service 進行分析。哪種解決方案可以滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company's application uses Network Load Balancers, Auto Scaling groups, Amazon EC2 instances, and databases that are deployed in an Amazon VPC. The company wants to capture information about traffic to and from the network interfaces in near real time in its Amazon VPC. The company wants to send the information to Amazon OpenSearch Service for analysis. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 在 Amazon CloudWatch Logs 中建立日誌組。配置VPC流日誌，將日誌資料傳送到日誌組。使用 Amazon Kinesis Data Streams 將日誌從日誌群組串流傳輸到 OpenSearch Service。
A.en: Create a log group in Amazon CloudWatch Logs. Configure VPC Flow Logs to send the log data to the log group. Use Amazon Kinesis Data Streams to stream the logs from the log group to OpenSearch Service.

B.zh: 在 Amazon CloudWatch Logs 中建立日誌組。配置VPC流日誌，將日誌資料傳送到日誌組。使用 Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose 將日誌從日誌群組串流傳輸到 OpenSearch Service。
B.en: Create a log group in Amazon CloudWatch Logs. Configure VPC Flow Logs to send the log data to the log group. Use Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose to stream the logs from the log group to OpenSearch Service.

C.zh: 在 AWS CloudTrail 中建立追蹤。配置VPC流日誌，將日誌資料傳送到路徑。使用 Amazon Kinesis Data Streams 將日誌從追蹤串流到 OpenSearch Service。
C.en: Create a trail in AWS CloudTrail. Configure VPC Flow Logs to send the log data to the trail. Use Amazon Kinesis Data Streams to stream the logs from the trail to OpenSearch Service.

D.zh: 在 AWS CloudTrail 中建立追蹤。配置VPC流日誌，將日誌資料傳送到路徑。使用 Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose 將日誌從追蹤串流到 OpenSearch Service。
D.en: Create a trail in AWS CloudTrail. Configure VPC Flow Logs to send the log data to the trail. Use Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose to stream the logs from the trail to OpenSearch Service.

A.zh: 錯誤。效能分析：VPC Flow Logs 可以將日誌傳送到 CloudWatch Logs，但使用 Amazon Kinesis Data Streams 進行串流處理並不是最佳選擇，因為 Kinesis Data Streams 需要額外的開發工作來處理資料流，而問題需要將日誌直接傳送到 OpenSearch Service。
A.en: Incorrect. Performance analysis: VPC Flow Logs can send logs to CloudWatch Logs, but using Amazon Kinesis Data Streams for streaming is not the best choice because Kinesis Data Streams requires additional development work to process the data stream, and the question requires sending logs directly to OpenSearch Service.

B.zh: 正確。效能分析：VPC Flow Logs 可以將日誌傳送到 CloudWatch Logs。 Kinesis Data Firehose 是一種託管服務，專門設計用於將資料串流傳輸到目標服務（例如 OpenSearch Service），支援無縫傳輸。這個選項是正確的。
B.en: Correct. Performance analysis: VPC Flow Logs can send logs to CloudWatch Logs. Kinesis Data Firehose is a hosting service specially designed to stream data to target services (such as OpenSearch Service), supporting seamless transmission. This option is correct.

C.zh: 錯誤。效能分析：VPC Flow Logs 無法直接將日誌傳送到 CloudTrail。 CloudTrail主要用於記錄API呼叫日誌，而非網路流量日誌。
C.en: Incorrect. Performance analysis: VPC Flow Logs cannot directly send logs to CloudTrail. CloudTrail is mainly used to record API call logs, not network traffic logs.

D.zh: 錯誤。效能分析：與選項C相同，VPC Flow Logs無法直接將日誌傳送到CloudTrail，因此此選項不符合第一步的要求。
D.en: Incorrect. Performance analysis: Same as option C, VPC Flow Logs cannot directly send logs to CloudTrail, so this option does not meet the requirements in the first step.

answer_text.zh: B. 在 Amazon CloudWatch Logs 中建立日誌組。配置VPC流日誌，將日誌資料傳送到日誌組。使用 Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose 將日誌從日誌群組串流傳輸到 OpenSearch Service。
answer_text.en: B. Create a log group in Amazon CloudWatch Logs. Configure VPC Flow Logs to send the log data to the log group. Use Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose to stream the logs from the log group to OpenSearch Service.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：B (90%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：效能分析：VPC Flow Logs 可以將日誌傳送到 CloudWatch Logs。 Kinesis Data Firehose 是一種託管服務，專門設計用於將資料串流傳輸到目標服務（例如 OpenSearch Service），支援無縫傳輸。這個選項是正確的。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (90%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Performance analysis: VPC Flow Logs can send logs to CloudWatch Logs. Kinesis Data Firehose is a hosting service specially designed to stream data to target services (such as OpenSearch Service), supporting seamless transmission. This option is correct.

questions.domain: 領域 4：設計成本優化架構 (Design Cost-Optimized Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司正在開發一個將在生產 Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS) 叢集上運行的應用程式。 EKS 叢集具有使用按需實例調配的託管節點群組。該公司需要一個專用的 EKS 叢集來進行開發工作。該公司將不經常使用開發集群來測試應用程式的彈性。 EKS 叢集必須管理所有節點。哪一種解決方案能夠最具成本效益地滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company is developing an application that will run on a production Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS) cluster. The EKS cluster has managed node groups that are provisioned with On-Demand Instances. The company needs a dedicated EKS cluster for development work. The company will use the development cluster infrequently to test the resiliency of the application. The EKS cluster must manage all the nodes. Which solution will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?

A.zh: 建立僅包含 Spot 執行個體的託管節點群組。
A.en: Create a managed node group that contains only Spot Instances.

B.zh: 建立兩個受管節點組。為一個節點組配置按需實例。使用 Spot 實例配置第二個節點組。
B.en: Create two managed node groups. Provision one node group with On-Demand Instances. Provision the second node group with Spot Instances.

C.zh: 建立一個具有使用 Spot 實例的啟動配置的 Auto Scaling 群組。配置使用者資料以將節點新增至 EKS 叢集。
C.en: Create an Auto Scaling group that has a launch configuration that uses Spot Instances. Configure the user data to add the nodes to the EKS cluster.

D.zh: 建立僅包含按需實例的受管節點群組。
D.en: Create a managed node group that contains only On-Demand Instances.

A.zh: 錯誤。僅限 Spot 的託管節點群組 成本最低；適合短期/可中斷的開發測試；完全符合「EKS 管理節點」（受管節點群組）。 Spot可能沒有容量，導致需要測試時無法拉起節點；而且一旦發生集中回收，叢集內可能沒有工作節點，影響測試連通性。它非常昂貴，但「需要時可用」的可靠性是有風險的，而且它可能不是「最划算的」（忽略可用性和營運成本）。
A.en: Incorrect. Spot-only Managed Node Group Lowest cost; suitable for short-term/interruptible development testing; fully compliant with "EKS Management Nodes" (managed node groups). Spot may have no capacity, resulting in nodes being unable to be pulled up when testing is required; and once centralized recycling occurs, there may be no working nodes within the cluster, affecting test connectivity. It is very expensive, but the reliability of "available when needed" is risky, and it may not be "MOST cost-effective" (ignoring both availability and operating costs).

B.zh: 正確。兩個被管節點組：1個On-Demand + 1個Spot 使用少量On-Demand作為基本容量，確保Spot拉伸時叢集仍能運行關鍵工作負載/基本組件；使用Spot承載彈性/非關鍵測試負載，顯著降低成本；仍然滿足「EKS管理所有節點」。邊界比A有更多的On-Demand成本。在「低頻使用但需要能夠隨時啟動進行韌性測試」的前提下，結合成本和可用性，這通常是最具成本效益的設計。
B.en: Correct. Two managed node groups: one On-Demand + one Spot Use a small amount of On-Demand as the basic capacity to ensure that the cluster can still run critical workloads/basic groupware when Spot is stretched; use Spot to carry elastic/non-critical test loads to significantly reduce costs; still meet the "EKS management of all nodes". The boundary has more On-Demand cost than A. Under the premise of "low frequency use but need to be able to start up for toughness testing at any time", combining cost and availability, this is usually the most cost-effective design.

C.zh: 錯誤。使用ASG+啟動設定（launchconfiguration）+使用者資料加入EKS這是自我管理的worker節點的做法。節點生命週期更多由您管理，而不是由EKS託管節點群組管理；且啟動配置也較舊（啟動範本現在更常見）。不符合「EKS叢集必須管理所有節點」的要求，因此不選擇。
C.en: Incorrect. Use ASG + launch configuration (launch configuration) + user data to join EKS This is the approach of self-managed worker nodes. The node life cycle is more managed by you, not by the EKS managed node group; and the launch configuration is also older (launch template is more common now). The requirement "The EKS cluster must manage all nodes" is not met, so it is not selected.

D.zh: 錯誤。受管節點組僅包含按需最穩定、可預測的容量；滿足「EKS管理所有節點」。開發集群使用頻率不高，全部按需使用，會造成明顯的浪費，並不是最划算的。成本優化不夠，所以不要選擇它。
D.en: Incorrect. Managed node group containing only On-Demand Most stable, predictable capacity; meets "EKS manages all nodes". The development cluster is used infrequently and is all on-demand, which will cause obvious waste and is not the most cost-effective. Not cost-optimized enough, so don’t choose it.

answer_text.zh: B. 建立兩個受管節點組。為一個節點組配置按需實例。使用 Spot 實例配置第二個節點組。
answer_text.en: B. Create two managed node groups. Provision one node group with On-Demand Instances. Provision the second node group with Spot Instances.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：B (50%)； A (48%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：兩個被管節點組：1個On-Demand + 1個Spot 使用少量On-Demand作為基本容量，確保Spot拉伸時叢集仍能運行關鍵工作負載/基本組件；使用Spot承載彈性/非關鍵測試負載，顯著降低成本；仍然滿足「EKS管理所有節點」。邊界比A有更多的On-Demand成本。在「低頻使用但需要能夠隨時啟動進行韌性測試」的前提下，結合成本和可用性，這通常是最具成本效益的設計。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (50%); A (48%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Two managed node groups: one On-Demand + one Spot Use a small amount of On-Demand as the basic capacity to ensure that the cluster can still run critical workloads/basic groupware when Spot is stretched; use Spot to carry elastic/non-critical test loads to significantly reduce costs; still meet the "EKS management of all nodes". The boundary has more On-Demand cost than A. Under the premise of "low frequency use but need to be able to start up for toughness testing at any time", combining cost and availability, this is usually the most cost-effective design.

questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司將敏感資料儲存在 Amazon S3 中。解決方案架構師需要建立加密解決方案。公司需要完全控制用戶以最少的努力創建、輪換和禁用加密密鑰的能力，以處理任何必須加密的資料。哪種解決方案可以滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company stores sensitive data in Amazon S3. A solutions architect needs to create an encryption solution. The company needs to fully control the ability of users to create, rotate, and disable encryption keys with minimal effort for any data that must be encrypted. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 使用帶有 Amazon S3 託管加密金鑰 (SSE-S3) 的預設伺服器端加密來儲存敏感資料。
A.en: Use default server-side encryption with Amazon S3 managed encryption keys (SSE-S3) to store the sensitive data.

B.zh: 使用 AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) 建立客戶託管金鑰。使用新金鑰透過使用 AWS KMS 金鑰 (SSE-KMS) 進行伺服器端加密來加密 S3 物件。
B.en: Create a customer managed key by using AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS). Use the new key to encrypt the S3 objects by using server-side encryption with AWS KMS keys (SSE-KMS).

C.zh: 使用 AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) 建立 AWS 託管金鑰。使用新金鑰透過使用 AWS KMS 金鑰 (SSE-KMS) 進行伺服器端加密來加密 S3 物件。
C.en: Create an AWS managed key by using AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS). Use the new key to encrypt the S3 objects by using server-side encryption with AWS KMS keys (SSE-KMS).

D.zh: 將 S3 物件下載到 Amazon EC2 執行個體。使用客戶管理的密鑰加密物件。將加密的物件上傳回 Amazon S3。
D.en: Download S3 objects to an Amazon EC2 instance. Encrypt the objects by using customer managed keys. Upload the encrypted objects back into Amazon S3.

A.zh: 錯誤。使用預設伺服器端加密和 Amazon S3 託管加密金鑰 (SSE-S3) 分析：此選項使用 Amazon S3 託管加密金鑰 (SSE-S3)，但公司需要完全控制建立、輪換和停用金鑰的能力。 SSE-S3不允許使用者完全控制金鑰，因此此選項不符合要求。
A.en: Incorrect. Use default server-side encryption and Amazon S3-managed encryption keys (SSE-S3) Analysis: This option uses Amazon S3-managed encryption keys (SSE-S3), but the company requires full control over the creation, rotation, and ability to disable keys. SSE-S3 does not allow the user to have full control over the keys, so this option does not meet the requirements.

B.zh: 正確。使用 AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) 建立客戶管理的金鑰，並使用新金鑰透過伺服器端加密和 AWS KMS 金鑰 (SSE-KMS) 加密 S3 物件 分析：此選項使用 AWS KMS 建立客戶管理的金鑰 (Customer Managed Key) 並使用 SSE-KMS 加密 S3 物件。客戶管理的密鑰允許用戶完全控制密鑰建立、輪換以及禁用密鑰的能力，符合公司要求。
B.en: Correct. Use AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) to create a customer-managed key and use the new key to encrypt the S3 object with server-side encryption and an AWS KMS key (SSE-KMS) Analysis: This option uses AWS KMS to create a customer-managed key (Customer Managed Key) and encrypt the S3 object with SSE-KMS. Customer-managed keys allow users full control over key creation, rotation and the ability to disable keys, consistent with company requirements.

C.zh: 錯誤。使用 AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) 建立 AWS 託管金鑰，並使用新金鑰透過伺服器端加密和 AWS KMS 金鑰 (SSE-KMS) 加密 S3 物件 分析：此選項使用 AWS 託管金鑰 (AWS Managed Key) 而不是客戶管理金鑰。 AWS 託管金鑰由 AWS 管理，無法讓使用者完全控制金鑰的建立、輪替以及停用金鑰的能力，因此不符合公司的要求。
C.en: Incorrect. Create an AWS Managed Key using AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) and use the new key to encrypt the S3 object with server-side encryption and an AWS KMS key (SSE-KMS) Analysis: This option uses an AWS Managed Key (AWS Managed Key) instead of a customer-managed key. AWS Managed Keys are managed by AWS and do not provide the user with full control over the creation, rotation, and ability to disable the key and therefore do not comply with the company's requirements.

D.zh: 錯誤。將 S3 物件下載到 Amazon EC2 實例，使用客戶管理的金鑰加密該對象，然後將加密的對象重新上傳到 Amazon S3 分析：此選項涉及手動加密過程，需要下載並重新上傳數據，從而增加了複雜性和成本。該公司需要最小努力的解決方案，因此此選項不符合資格。
D.en: Incorrect. Download the S3 object to an Amazon EC2 instance, encrypt the object using a customer-managed key, and then re-upload the encrypted object to Amazon S3 Analysis: This option involves a manual encryption process that requires downloading and re-uploading the data, adding complexity and cost. The company requires a minimum effort solution, so this option does not qualify.

answer_text.zh: B. 使用 AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) 建立客戶託管金鑰。使用新金鑰透過使用 AWS KMS 金鑰 (SSE-KMS) 進行伺服器端加密來加密 S3 物件。
answer_text.en: B. Create a customer managed key by using AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS). Use the new key to encrypt the S3 objects by using server-side encryption with AWS KMS keys (SSE-KMS).

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：B (96%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：使用 AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) 建立客戶管理的金鑰，並使用新金鑰透過伺服器端加密和 AWS KMS 金鑰 (SSE-KMS) 加密 S3 物件 分析：此選項使用 AWS KMS 建立客戶管理的金鑰 (Customer Managed Key) 並使用 SSE-KMS 加密 S3 物件。客戶管理的密鑰允許用戶完全控制密鑰建立、輪換以及禁用密鑰的能力，符合公司要求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (96%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Use AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) to create a customer-managed key and use the new key to encrypt the S3 object with server-side encryption and an AWS KMS key (SSE-KMS) Analysis: This option uses AWS KMS to create a customer-managed key (Customer Managed Key) and encrypt the S3 object with SSE-KMS. Customer-managed keys allow users full control over key creation, rotation and the ability to disable keys, consistent with company requirements.

questions.domain: 領域 1：設計彈性架構 (Design Resilient Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司希望將其本機虛擬機器 (VM) 備份到 AWS。該公司的備份解決方案將本地備份作為物件匯出到 Amazon S3 儲存桶。 S3 備份必須保留 30 天，並且必須在 30 天後自動刪除。哪一種步驟組合可以滿足這些要求？ （選擇三項。）
question_text.en: A company wants to back up its on-premises virtual machines (VMs) to AWS. The company's backup solution exports on-premises backups to an Amazon S3 bucket as objects. The S3 backups must be retained for 30 days and must be automatically deleted after 30 days. Which combination of steps will meet these requirements? (Choose three.)

A.zh: 建立啟用了 S3 物件鎖定的 S3 儲存桶。
A.en: Create an S3 bucket that has S3 Object Lock enabled.

B.zh: 建立啟用了物件版本控制的 S3 儲存桶。
B.en: Create an S3 bucket that has object versioning enabled.

C.zh: 為物件配置 30 天的預設保留期。
C.en: Configure a default retention period of 30 days for the objects.

D.zh: 設定 S3 生命週期策略以保護物件 30 天。
D.en: Configure an S3 Lifecycle policy to protect the objects for 30 days.

E.zh: 設定 S3 生命週期策略，使物件在 30 天後過期。
E.en: Configure an S3 Lifecycle policy to expire the objects after 30 days.

F.zh: 設定備份解決方案以標記具有 30 天保留期的對象
F.en: Configure the backup solution to tag the objects with a 30-day retention period

A.zh: 正確。。 S3物件鎖可以保證物件在指定的保留期限內不被刪除或覆蓋，滿足問題所需的備份保護要求。
A.en: Correct. . S3 Object Lock can ensure that objects cannot be deleted or overwritten within the specified retention period, meeting the backup protection requirements required by the problem.

B.zh: 錯誤。。物件版本控制主要用於保留物件的多個版本，但是問題中並沒有提到需要版本控制功能。
B.en: Incorrect. . Object version control is mainly used to retain multiple versions of objects, but the question does not mention the need for version control functions.

C.zh: 正確。。配置預設的30天保留期可確保物件在30天內受到保護並符合問題要求。
C.en: Correct. . Configuring the default 30-day retention period ensures that objects are protected within 30 days and meets the question requirements.

D.zh: 錯誤。。生命週期策略用於管理物件的儲存和刪除，而不是保護物件不被刪除。
D.en: Incorrect. . Lifecycle policies are used to manage the storage and deletion of objects, not to protect objects from deletion.

E.zh: 正確。。生命週期策略可以自動刪除對象，如果滿足問題要求，30天後自動刪除。
E.en: Correct. . The life cycle policy can automatically delete objects and automatically delete them after 30 days if they meet the requirements of the question.

F.zh: 錯誤。。標題並沒有提到需要透過標籤來實現保留期限管理，而且標籤本身並不能直接保護或刪除物件。
F.en: Incorrect. . The title does not mention the need to implement retention period management through tags, and tags themselves cannot directly protect or delete objects.

answer_text.zh: A. 建立啟用了 S3 物件鎖定的 S3 儲存桶。；C. 為物件配置 30 天的預設保留期。；E. 設定 S3 生命週期策略，使物件在 30 天後過期。
answer_text.en: A. Create an S3 bucket that has S3 Object Lock enabled.; C. Configure a default retention period of 30 days for the objects.; E. Configure an S3 Lifecycle policy to expire the objects after 30 days.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：ACE (63%)； ADE (26%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：。 S3物件鎖可以保證物件在指定的保留期限內不被刪除或覆蓋，滿足問題所需的備份保護要求。；。配置預設的30天保留期可確保物件在30天內受到保護並符合問題要求。；。生命週期策略可以自動刪除對象，如果滿足問題要求，30天後自動刪除。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: ACE (63%); ADE (26%). Popular discussion and key point summary: . S3 Object Lock can ensure that objects cannot be deleted or overwritten within the specified retention period, meeting the backup protection requirements required by the problem.; . Configuring the default 30-day retention period ensures that objects are protected within 30 days and meets the question requirements.; . The life cycle policy can automatically delete objects and automatically delete them after 30 days if they meet the requirements of the question.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 解決方案架構師需要將檔案從 Amazon S3 儲存桶複製到 Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) 檔案系統和另一個 S3 儲存桶。必須連續複製文件。新檔案會一致加入到原始 S3 儲存桶中。僅當來源檔案發生變更時，才應覆蓋複製的檔案。哪種解決方案能夠以最少的營運開銷滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A solutions architect needs to copy files from an Amazon S3 bucket to an Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) file system and another S3 bucket. The files must be copied continuously. New files are added to the original S3 bucket consistently. The copied files should be overwritten only if the source file changes. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

A.zh: 為目標 S3 儲存桶和 EFS 檔案系統建立 AWS DataSync 位置。為目標 S3 儲存桶和 EFS 檔案系統建立任務。將傳輸模式設定為僅傳輸已變更的資料。
A.en: Create an AWS DataSync location for both the destination S3 bucket and the EFS file system. Create a task for the destination S3 bucket and the EFS file system. Set the transfer mode to transfer only data that has changed.

B.zh: 建立 AWS Lambda 函數。將檔案系統掛載到該函數。設定 S3 事件通知，以便在 Amazon S3 中建立和變更檔案時呼叫函數。設定將檔案複製到檔案系統和目標S3儲存桶的功能。
B.en: Create an AWS Lambda function. Mount the file system to the function. Set up an S3 event notification to invoke the function when files are created and changed in Amazon S3. Configure the function to copy files to the file system and the destination S3 bucket.

C.zh: 為目標 S3 儲存桶和 EFS 檔案系統建立 AWS DataSync 位置。為目標 S3 儲存桶和 EFS 檔案系統建立任務。設定傳輸模式以傳輸所有資料。
C.en: Create an AWS DataSync location for both the destination S3 bucket and the EFS file system. Create a task for the destination S3 bucket and the EFS file system. Set the transfer mode to transfer all data.

D.zh: 在與檔案系統相同的 VPC 中啟動 Amazon EC2 執行個體。掛載檔案系統。建立一個腳本，定期將來源 S3 儲存桶中變更的所有物件同步到目標 S3 儲存桶和已掛載的檔案系統。
D.en: Launch an Amazon EC2 instance in the same VPC as the file system. Mount the file system. Create a script to routinely synchronize all objects that changed in the origin S3 bucket to the destination S3 bucket and the mounted file system.

A.zh: 正確。分析：AWS DataSync是一項專為高效同步資料而設計的服務，支援S3和EFS之間的資料傳輸。將傳輸模式設定為「僅傳輸變更的資料」可以滿足問題的要求，即僅覆蓋來源檔案中的變更。此方案運作成本較低，完全滿足問題要求。
A.en: Correct. Analysis: AWS DataSync is a service specifically designed to efficiently synchronize data, supporting data transfer between S3 and EFS. Setting the transfer mode to "Only transfer changed data" can meet the requirements of the question, that is, only the changes in the source file will be overwritten. This option has low operating cost and fully meets the requirements of the question.

B.zh: 錯誤。分析：使用Lambda函數和S3事件處理程序可以實現檔案複製，但需要手動編寫程式碼來處理邏輯。將檔案裝載到 Lambda 函數中會增加複雜性和操作開銷。此選項的操作成本較高，且不是最佳選擇。
B.en: Incorrect. Analysis: Using Lambda functions and S3 event handlers can achieve file copying, but you need to manually write code to handle the logic. Mounting files into Lambda functions can add complexity and operational overhead. This option is expensive to operate and is not the best choice.

C.zh: 錯誤。分析：將傳輸模式設為「傳輸所有資料」會導致每次都複製所有文件，而不僅僅是更改的文件。此選項不滿足問題的要求，因為它增加了不必要的傳輸開銷。
C.en: Incorrect. Analysis: Setting the transfer mode to "Transfer all data" will cause all files to be copied every time, not only the changed files. This option does not meet the requirements of the question because it adds unnecessary transmission overhead.

D.zh: 錯誤。分析：使用EC2實例和腳本可以實現檔案同步，但需要手動編寫和維護腳本。此選項的操作成本較高，且不是最佳選擇。
D.en: Incorrect. Analysis: File synchronization can be achieved using EC2 instances and scripts, but requires manual writing and maintenance of scripts. This option is expensive to operate and is not the best choice.

answer_text.zh: A. 為目標 S3 儲存桶和 EFS 檔案系統建立 AWS DataSync 位置。為目標 S3 儲存桶和 EFS 檔案系統建立任務。將傳輸模式設定為僅傳輸已變更的資料。
answer_text.en: A. Create an AWS DataSync location for both the destination S3 bucket and the EFS file system. Create a task for the destination S3 bucket and the EFS file system. Set the transfer mode to transfer only data that has changed.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：A (100%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：分析：AWS DataSync是一項專為高效同步資料而設計的服務，支援S3和EFS之間的資料傳輸。將傳輸模式設定為「僅傳輸變更的資料」可以滿足問題的要求，即僅覆蓋來源檔案中的變更。此方案運作成本較低，完全滿足問題要求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Analysis: AWS DataSync is a service specifically designed to efficiently synchronize data, supporting data transfer between S3 and EFS. Setting the transfer mode to "Only transfer changed data" can meet the requirements of the question, that is, only the changes in the source file will be overwritten. This option has low operating cost and fully meets the requirements of the question.

questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司使用 Amazon EC2 執行個體並將資料儲存在 Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) 磁碟區上。本公司必須確保使用 AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) 對所有靜態資料進行加密。公司必須能夠控制加密金鑰的輪換。哪種解決方案能夠以最少的營運開銷滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company uses Amazon EC2 instances and stores data on Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) volumes. The company must ensure that all data is encrypted at rest by using AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS). The company must be able to control rotation of the encryption keys. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

A.zh: 建立客戶管理的金鑰。使用密鑰加密 EBS 磁碟區。
A.en: Create a customer managed key. Use the key to encrypt the EBS volumes.

B.zh: 使用 AWS 託管金鑰加密 EBS 磁碟區。使用金鑰配置自動金鑰輪換。
B.en: Use an AWS managed key to encrypt the EBS volumes. Use the key to configure automatic key rotation.

C.zh: 使用匯入的金鑰材料建立外部 KMS 金鑰。使用密鑰加密 EBS 磁碟區。
C.en: Create an external KMS key with imported key material. Use the key to encrypt the EBS volumes.

D.zh: 使用 AWS 擁有的金鑰來加密 EBS 磁碟區。
D.en: Use an AWS owned key to encrypt the EBS volumes.

A.zh: 正確。客戶管理金鑰是由使用者建立和管理的 KMS 金鑰。使用者可以完全控制密鑰的生命週期，包括啟用密鑰輪換。合規：使用者可以控制金鑰輪換並確保資料加密符合要求。營運開銷低：AWS 提供自動金鑰輪換，使用者只需啟用即可。
A.en: Correct. Customer Managed Key is a KMS key created and managed by the user. The user has full control over the life cycle of the key, including enabling key rotation. Compliant: Users can control key rotation and ensure data encryption complies with requirements. Low operational overhead: AWS provides automated key rotation that users only need to enable.

B.zh: 錯誤。AWS 託管金鑰是由 AWS 建立和管理的金鑰，使用者無法控制金鑰輪替。不符合要求：問題明確要求使用者控制金鑰輪換，但AWS託管金鑰不支援使用者控制輪替。
B.en: Incorrect. AWS Managed Keys are keys created and managed by AWS, and the user has no control over key rotation. Does not meet the requirements: The question clearly requires users to control key rotation, but AWS managed keys do not support user-controlled rotation.

C.zh: 錯誤。雖然導入的金鑰材料可用於加密，但這會增加操作複雜性，因為使用者需要管理金鑰材料的導入和生命週期。不符合要求：此問題需要最小的營運開銷，導入關鍵材料會增加管理複雜性。
C.en: Incorrect. Although imported key material can be used for encryption, this increases operational complexity as the user needs to manage the import and lifecycle of the key material. Not meeting the requirements: The question requires minimal operational overhead and importing key material increases management complexity.

D.zh: 錯誤。AWS 擁有的金鑰完全由 AWS 管理，使用者無法存取或控制它們。不符合要求：問題要求使用者控制密鑰輪換，但AWS擁有的密鑰不支援用戶控制。
D.en: Incorrect. AWS Owned Keys are fully AWS-managed and the user has no access or control over them. Not meeting the requirements: The question requires users to control key rotation, but the keys owned by AWS do not support user control.

answer_text.zh: A. 建立客戶管理的金鑰。使用密鑰加密 EBS 磁碟區。
answer_text.en: A. Create a customer managed key. Use the key to encrypt the EBS volumes.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：A (88%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：客戶管理金鑰是由使用者建立和管理的 KMS 金鑰。使用者可以完全控制密鑰的生命週期，包括啟用密鑰輪換。合規：使用者可以控制金鑰輪換並確保資料加密符合要求。營運開銷低：AWS 提供自動金鑰輪換，使用者只需啟用即可。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (88%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Customer Managed Key is a KMS key created and managed by the user. The user has full control over the life cycle of the key, including enabling key rotation. Compliant: Users can control key rotation and ensure data encryption complies with requirements. Low operational overhead: AWS provides automated key rotation that users only need to enable.

questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 公司需要一個解決方案來在 Amazon EC2 執行個體上強制執行靜態資料加密。此解決方案必須自動識別不合規的資源並根據結果強制執行合規策略。哪種解決方案能夠以最少的管理開銷來滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company needs a solution to enforce data encryption at rest on Amazon EC2 instances. The solution must automatically identify noncompliant resources and enforce compliance policies on findings. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST administrative overhead?

A.zh: 使用 IAM 策略，該策略允許使用者僅建立加密的 Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) 磁碟區。使用 AWS Config 和 AWS Systems Manager 自動偵測和修復未加密的 EBS 磁碟區。
A.en: Use an IAM policy that allows users to create only encrypted Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) volumes. Use AWS Config and AWS Systems Manager to automate the detection and remediation of unencrypted EBS volumes.

B.zh: 使用 AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) 管理對加密的 Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) 磁碟區的存取。使用 AWS Lambda 和 Amazon EventBridge 自動偵測並修復未加密的 EBS 磁碟區。
B.en: Use AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) to manage access to encrypted Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) volumes. Use AWS Lambda and Amazon EventBridge to automate the detection and remediation of unencrypted EBS volumes.

C.zh: 使用 Amazon Macie 偵測未加密的 Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) 磁碟區。使用 AWS Systems Manager 自動化規則自動加密現有和新的 EBS 磁碟區。
C.en: Use Amazon Macie to detect unencrypted Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) volumes. Use AWS Systems Manager Automation rules to automatically encrypt existing and new EBS volumes.

D.zh: 使用 Amazon Inspector 偵測未加密的 Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) 磁碟區。使用 AWS Systems Manager 自動化規則自動加密現有和新的 EBS 磁碟區。
D.en: Use Amazon inspector to detect unencrypted Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) volumes. Use AWS Systems Manager Automation rules to automatically encrypt existing and new EBS volumes.

A.zh: 正確。IAM 策略可防止建立未加密的 EBS 卷，AWS Config 持續偵測不合規情況，Systems Manager Automation 以較低的管理開銷修復發現的問題。
A.en: Correct. An IAM policy prevents creation of unencrypted EBS volumes, AWS Config continuously detects noncompliance, and Systems Manager Automation remediates findings with low administrative overhead.

B.zh: 錯誤。使用 AWS KMS、AWS Lambda 和 Amazon EventBridge：錯誤：儘管 AWS KMS 可以管理加密金鑰，但它無法偵測未加密的 EBS 磁碟區。錯誤：使用 AWS Lambda 和 EventBridge 需要額外的自訂程式碼和事件規則，這會增加管理開銷。
B.en: Incorrect. Using AWS KMS, AWS Lambda, and Amazon EventBridge: Error: Although AWS KMS can manage encryption keys, it has no way of detecting unencrypted EBS volumes. Error: Using AWS Lambda and EventBridge requires additional custom code and event rules, which increases management overhead.

C.zh: 錯誤。Amazon Macie 發現 S3 中的敏感數據，但不評估 EBS 磁碟區加密，因此無法提供所需的合規性調查結果。
C.en: Incorrect. Amazon Macie discovers sensitive data in S3 and does not evaluate EBS volume encryption, so it cannot supply the required compliance findings.

D.zh: 錯誤。Amazon Inspector 評估工作負載漏洞和暴露情況；它不是用於偵測未加密的 EBS 磁碟區的託管設定規則服務。
D.en: Incorrect. Amazon Inspector assesses workload vulnerabilities and exposure; it is not the managed configuration rule service for detecting unencrypted EBS volumes.

answer_text.zh: A. 使用 IAM 策略，該策略允許使用者僅建立加密的 Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) 磁碟區。使用 AWS Config 和 AWS Systems Manager 自動偵測和修復未加密的 EBS 磁碟區。
answer_text.en: A. Use an IAM policy that allows users to create only encrypted Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) volumes. Use AWS Config and AWS Systems Manager to automate the detection and remediation of unencrypted EBS volumes.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：A (96%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：IAM 策略可防止建立未加密的 EBS 卷，AWS Config 持續偵測不合規情況，Systems Manager Automation 以較低的管理開銷修復發現的問題。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (96%). Popular discussion and key point summary: An IAM policy prevents creation of unencrypted EBS volumes, AWS Config continuously detects noncompliance, and Systems Manager Automation remediates findings with low administrative overhead.

questions.domain: 領域 1：設計彈性架構 (Design Resilient Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司正在將其多層本地應用程式遷移到 AWS。該應用程式由單節點 MySQL 資料庫和多節點 Web 層組成。公司必須盡量減少遷移期間對應用程式的變更。該公司希望在遷移後提高應用程式的彈性。哪一種步驟組合可以滿足這些要求？ （選兩個。）
question_text.en: A company is migrating its multi-tier on-premises application to AWS. The application consists of a single-node MySQL database and a multi-node web tier. The company must minimize changes to the application during the migration. The company wants to improve application resiliency after the migration. Which combination of steps will meet these requirements? (Choose two.)

A.zh: 將 Web 層移轉到 Application Load Balancer 後面的 Auto Scaling 群組中的 Amazon EC2 執行個體。
A.en: Migrate the web tier to Amazon EC2 instances in an Auto Scaling group behind an Application Load Balancer.

B.zh: 將資料庫移轉到網路負載平衡器後面的 Auto Scaling 群組中的 Amazon EC2 執行個體。
B.en: Migrate the database to Amazon EC2 instances in an Auto Scaling group behind a Network Load Balancer.

C.zh: 將資料庫移轉到 Amazon RDS 多可用區部署。
C.en: Migrate the database to an Amazon RDS Multi-AZ deployment.

D.zh: 將 Web 層遷移到 AWS Lambda 函數。
D.en: Migrate the web tier to an AWS Lambda function.

E.zh: 將資料庫移轉到 Amazon DynamoDB 表。
E.en: Migrate the database to an Amazon DynamoDB table.

A.zh: 正確。將 Web 層遷移到 Auto Scaling 群組中的 EC2 執行個體並將其放置在 Application Load Balancer 後面可以提高 Web 層的彈性和可用性。 Auto Scaling群組可以根據流量動態調整執行個體數量，而ALB可以分配流量並提供高可用性。
A.en: Correct. Migrating the web tier to EC2 instances in an Auto Scaling group and placing it behind an Application Load Balancer can improve the resiliency and availability of the web tier. Auto Scaling groups can dynamically adjust the number of instances based on traffic, while ALB can distribute traffic and provide high availability.

B.zh: 錯誤。資料庫通常不適合放置在 Auto Scaling 群組中，因為資料庫需要網路共享，而 Auto Scaling 群組中的執行個體是不受網路限制的。網路負載平衡器適合處理高效能網路流量，但不適合直接用於高可用性資料庫。
B.en: Incorrect. Databases are usually not suitable to be placed in Auto Scaling groups because databases require tethering, and instances in Auto Scaling groups are tetherless. Network Load Balancer is suitable for handling high-performance network traffic, but is not suitable for direct use in high-availability databases.

C.zh: 正確。Amazon RDS多可用區部署為資料庫提供了高可用性和災難復原能力，滿足問題的要求。 RDS可以直接支援MySQL，無需對應用程式進行重大修改。
C.en: Correct. Amazon RDS Multi-AZ deployment provides high availability and disaster recovery capabilities for the database, which meets the requirements of the question. RDS can directly support MySQL without major modifications to the application.

D.zh: 錯誤。AWS Lambda 適用於無伺服器架構，但問題應該是「盡量減少對應用程式的修改」。將 Web 層遷移到 Lambda 需要對應用程式進行重大變更。
D.en: Incorrect. AWS Lambda is suitable for serverless architecture, but the question should be "minimize modifications to the application". Migrating the web layer to Lambda requires significant changes to the application.

E.zh: 錯誤。DynamoDB是NoSQL資料庫，問題中的資料庫是MySQL（關聯式資料庫）。遷移到 DynamoDB 需要對應用程式進行重大修改。
E.en: Incorrect. DynamoDB is a NoSQL database, and the database in the question is MySQL (relational database). Migrating to DynamoDB requires significant modifications to the application.

answer_text.zh: A. 將 Web 層移轉到 Application Load Balancer 後面的 Auto Scaling 群組中的 Amazon EC2 執行個體。；C. 將資料庫移轉到 Amazon RDS 多可用區部署。
answer_text.en: A. Migrate the web tier to Amazon EC2 instances in an Auto Scaling group behind an Application Load Balancer.; C. Migrate the database to an Amazon RDS Multi-AZ deployment.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：AC (100%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：將 Web 層遷移到 Auto Scaling 群組中的 EC2 執行個體並將其放置在 Application Load Balancer 後面可以提高 Web 層的彈性和可用性。 Auto Scaling群組可以根據流量動態調整執行個體數量，而ALB可以分配流量並提供高可用性。；Amazon RDS多可用區部署為資料庫提供了高可用性和災難復原能力，滿足問題的要求。 RDS可以直接支援MySQL，無需對應用程式進行重大修改。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: AC (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Migrating the web tier to EC2 instances in an Auto Scaling group and placing it behind an Application Load Balancer can improve the resiliency and availability of the web tier. Auto Scaling groups can dynamically adjust the number of instances based on traffic, while ALB can distribute traffic and provide high availability.; Amazon RDS Multi-AZ deployment provides high availability and disaster recovery capabilities for the database, which meets the requirements of the question. RDS can directly support MySQL without major modifications to the application.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司希望將其 Web 應用程式從本地遷移到 AWS。該公司位於 eu-central-1 地區附近。由於法規原因，該公司無法在 eu-central-1 中啟動其部分應用程式。該公司希望實現個位數毫秒的延遲。哪種解決方案可以滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company wants to migrate its web applications from on premises to AWS. The company is located close to the eu-central-1 Region. Because of regulations, the company cannot launch some of its applications in eu-central-1. The company wants to achieve single-digit millisecond latency. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 在 eu-central-1 中部署應用程式。將該公司的 VPC 從 eu-central-1 擴展到 Amazon CloudFront 中的邊緣站點。
A.en: Deploy the applications in eu-central-1. Extend the company’s VPC from eu-central-1 to an edge location in Amazon CloudFront.

B.zh: 透過將公司的 VPC 從 eu-central-1 擴展到所選本地區域，在 AWS 本地區域中部署應用程式。
B.en: Deploy the applications in AWS Local Zones by extending the company's VPC from eu-central-1 to the chosen Local Zone.

C.zh: 在 eu-central-1 中部署應用程式。將公司的 VPC 從 eu-central-1 擴展到 Amazon CloudFront 中的區域邊緣快取。
C.en: Deploy the applications in eu-central-1. Extend the company’s VPC from eu-central-1 to the regional edge caches in Amazon CloudFront.

D.zh: 透過將公司的 VPC 從 eu-central-1 擴展到所選的 Wavelength 區域，在 AWS Wavelength 區域中部署應用程式。
D.en: Deploy the applications in AWS Wavelength Zones by extending the company’s VPC from eu-central-1 to the chosen Wavelength Zone.

A.zh: 錯誤。CloudFront 的邊緣網站主要用於內容交付 (CDN)，而不是用於託管應用程式。它無法滿足個位數毫秒延遲要求。
A.en: Incorrect. CloudFront's edge locations are primarily used for content delivery (CDN), not for hosting applications. It cannot meet single-digit millisecond latency requirements.

B.zh: 正確。AWS 本地擴展區允許使用者在靠近最終用戶的區域中運行應用程序，從而實現低延遲（個位數毫秒）。這符合題目要求。
B.en: Correct. AWS Local Zones allow users to run applications in a zone close to end users, resulting in low latency (single-digit milliseconds). This meets the question requirements.

C.zh: 錯誤。CloudFront 的區域邊緣快取主要旨在優化內容分發的效能，而不是託管應用程式。它無法滿足個位數毫秒延遲要求。
C.en: Incorrect. CloudFront's regional edge cache is primarily designed to optimize the performance of content distribution, not hosting applications. It cannot meet single-digit millisecond latency requirements.

D.zh: 錯誤。AWS Wavelength Zones 主要應用於 5G 網路的邊緣運算場景，適合行動裝置的低延遲需求。該問題沒有提及與5G或行動裝置相關的要求，因此不適合這種場景。
D.en: Incorrect. AWS Wavelength Zones is mainly used in edge computing scenarios of 5G networks and is suitable for the low-latency requirements of mobile devices. The question does not mention requirements related to 5G or mobile devices, so it is not suitable for this scenario.

answer_text.zh: B. 透過將公司的 VPC 從 eu-central-1 擴展到所選本地區域，在 AWS 本地區域中部署應用程式。
answer_text.en: B. Deploy the applications in AWS Local Zones by extending the company's VPC from eu-central-1 to the chosen Local Zone.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：B (77%)； D (23%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：AWS 本地擴展區允許使用者在靠近最終用戶的區域中運行應用程序，從而實現低延遲（個位數毫秒）。這符合題目要求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (77%); D (23%). Popular discussion and key point summary: AWS Local Zones allow users to run applications in a zone close to end users, resulting in low latency (single-digit milliseconds). This meets the question requirements.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 某公司的電子商務網站流量無法預測，並使用 AWS Lambda 函數直接存取私有 Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL 資料庫執行個體。該公司希望維持可預測的資料庫效能，並確保 Lambda 呼叫不會因連接過多而導致資料庫過載。解決方案架構師應該怎麼做才能滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company’s ecommerce website has unpredictable traffic and uses AWS Lambda functions to directly access a private Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL DB instance. The company wants to maintain predictable database performance and ensure that the Lambda invocations do not overload the database with too many connections. What should a solutions architect do to meet these requirements?

A.zh: 將客戶端驅動程式指向 RDS 自訂端點。在 VPC 內部署 Lambda 函數。
A.en: Point the client driver at an RDS custom endpoint. Deploy the Lambda functions inside a VPC.

B.zh: 將客戶端驅動程式指向 RDS 代理端點。在 VPC 內部署 Lambda 函數。
B.en: Point the client driver at an RDS proxy endpoint. Deploy the Lambda functions inside a VPC.

C.zh: 將客戶端驅動程式指向 RDS 自訂端點。在 VPC 外部部署 Lambda 函數。
C.en: Point the client driver at an RDS custom endpoint. Deploy the Lambda functions outside a VPC.

D.zh: 將客戶端驅動程式指向 RDS 代理端點。在 VPC 外部部署 Lambda 函數。
D.en: Point the client driver at an RDS proxy endpoint. Deploy the Lambda functions outside a VPC.

A.zh: 錯誤。RDS自訂端點無法解決資料庫連線過載問題。主要用於配置特定的讀寫流量，但不管理連線池。正確：在 VPC 內部署 Lambda 函數非常重要，因為 RDS 執行個體通常位於 VPC 內。
A.en: Incorrect. RDS custom endpoint cannot solve the database connection overload problem. It is mainly used to configure specific read and write traffic, but does not manage connection pools. Correct: It is important to deploy the Lambda function inside a VPC because RDS instances are usually located inside a VPC.

B.zh: 正確。RDS Proxy 可以管理資料庫連接池，減少 Lambda 函數對資料庫的直接連接壓力，從而避免過載。正確：在 VPC 內部署 Lambda 函數非常重要，因為 RDS 執行個體通常位於 VPC 內。
B.en: Correct. RDS Proxy can manage the database connection pool, reducing the direct connection pressure of the Lambda function on the database, thereby avoiding overload. Correct: It is important to deploy the Lambda function inside a VPC because RDS instances are usually located inside a VPC.

C.zh: 錯誤。RDS自訂端點無法解決資料庫連線過載問題。錯誤：部署在VPC外部的Lambda函數無法直接存取RDS實例，除非透過複雜的網路配置（例如NAT網關），這不符合最佳實務。
C.en: Incorrect. RDS custom endpoint cannot solve the database connection overload problem. Error: Lambda functions deployed outside the VPC cannot directly access RDS instances unless through complex network configuration (such as NAT gateway), which is not in line with best practices.

D.zh: 錯誤。RDS Proxy 可以管理資料庫連線池，減少 Lambda 函數對資料庫的直連壓力。錯誤：部署在VPC外部的Lambda函數無法直接存取RDS實例，除非透過複雜的網路配置（例如NAT網關），這不符合最佳實務。
D.en: Incorrect. RDS Proxy can manage the database connection pool and reduce the direct connection pressure of the Lambda function on the database. Error: Lambda functions deployed outside the VPC cannot directly access RDS instances unless through complex network configuration (such as NAT gateway), which is not in line with best practices.

answer_text.zh: B. 將客戶端驅動程式指向 RDS 代理端點。在 VPC 內部署 Lambda 函數。
answer_text.en: B. Point the client driver at an RDS proxy endpoint. Deploy the Lambda functions inside a VPC.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：B (100%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：RDS Proxy 可以管理資料庫連接池，減少 Lambda 函數對資料庫的直接連接壓力，從而避免過載。正確：在 VPC 內部署 Lambda 函數非常重要，因為 RDS 執行個體通常位於 VPC 內。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: RDS Proxy can manage the database connection pool, reducing the direct connection pressure of the Lambda function on the database, thereby avoiding overload. Correct: It is important to deploy the Lambda function inside a VPC because RDS instances are usually located inside a VPC.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司正在創建一個應用程式。該公司將應用程式測試的資料儲存在多個本地位置。該公司需要將本地位置連接到 AWS 雲端中 AWS 區域中的 VPC。明年帳戶和 VPC 的數量將會增加。網路架構必須簡化新連接的管理，並且必須提供擴展能力。哪種解決方案能夠以最少的管理開銷來滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company is creating an application. The company stores data from tests of the application in multiple on-premises locations. The company needs to connect the on-premises locations to VPCs in an AWS Region in the AWS Cloud. The number of accounts and VPCs will increase during the next year. The network architecture must simplify the administration of new connections and must provide the ability to scale. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST administrative overhead?

A.zh: 在 VPC 之間建立對等連線。在 VPC 和本地位置之間建立 VPN 連線。
A.en: Create a peering connection between the VPCs. Create a VPN connection between the VPCs and the on-premises locations.

B.zh: 啟動 Amazon EC2 執行個體。在實例上，包括使用 VPN 連線來連接所有 VPC 和本地位置的 VPN 軟體。
B.en: Launch an Amazon EC2 instance. On the instance, include VPN software that uses a VPN connection to connect all VPCs and on-premises locations.

C.zh: 建立中轉網關。為 VPC 連線建立 VPC 連線。為本機連線建立 VPN 附件。
C.en: Create a transit gateway. Create VPC attachments for the VPC connections. Create VPN attachments for the on-premises connections.

D.zh: 在本機位置和中央 VPC 之間建立 AWS Direct Connect 連線。透過對等連接將中心VPC與其他VPC連接起來。
D.en: Create an AWS Direct Connect connection between the on-premises locations and a central VPC. Connect the central VPC to other VPCs by using peering connections.

A.zh: 錯誤。對等互連（VPC Peering）適用於兩個 VPC 之間的直接連接，但隨著 VPC 數量的增加，管理多個對等連接變得複雜。 VPN 連線可以連接本地位置和 VPC，但不能簡化多個 VPC 之間的連線管理。此選項無法滿足「簡化管理」和「可擴展性」的要求，因此是一個錯誤。
A.en: Incorrect. Peering (VPC Peering) is suitable for direct connections between two VPCs, but as the number of VPCs increases, managing multiple peering connections becomes complex. VPN connections can connect on-premises locations and VPCs, but they do not simplify connection management between multiple VPCs. This option fails to meet the requirements of "simplified management" and "scalability" and is therefore an error.

B.zh: 錯誤。使用 EC2 執行個體執行 VPN 軟體是一種手動管理的解決方案，會增加操作複雜度。此方式不具備自動伸縮能力，需要額外的實例管理工作。此選項無法滿足「最小管理開銷」要求，因此是一個錯誤。
B.en: Incorrect. Using EC2 instances to run VPN software is a manually managed solution that increases operational complexity. This method does not have automatic scaling capabilities and requires additional instance management work. This option fails to meet the "minimum administrative overhead" requirement and is therefore an error.

C.zh: 正確。是一種集中式網路連接解決方案，可簡化多個 VPC 和本地位置之間的連接管理。透過VPC附著和VPN附著，可以輕鬆擴展網路架構，滿足未來的擴展需求。此選項滿足「簡化管理」和「可擴展性」的要求，因此是正確的。
C.en: Correct. is a centralized network connectivity solution that simplifies management of connectivity between multiple VPCs and on-premises locations. Through VPC attachments and VPN attachments, the network architecture can be easily expanded to meet future expansion needs. This option meets the requirements of "simplified management" and "scalability" and is therefore correct.

D.zh: 錯誤。專線連接是一種高效能連接方法，但它僅適用於本地位置和中心 VPC 之間。使用對等互連連接其他 VPC 會增加管理複雜性，尤其是隨著 VPC 數量的增加。此選項無法滿足「簡化管理」的要求，因此是一個錯誤。
D.en: Incorrect. Direct Connect is a high-performance connection method, but it only works between local locations and a central VPC. Using peering to connect other VPCs increases management complexity, especially as the number of VPCs increases. This option fails to meet the requirements of "simplified administration" and is therefore an error.

answer_text.zh: C. 建立中轉網關。為 VPC 連線建立 VPC 連線。為本機連線建立 VPN 附件。
answer_text.en: C. Create a transit gateway. Create VPC attachments for the VPC connections. Create VPN attachments for the on-premises connections.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：C (100%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：是一種集中式網路連接解決方案，可簡化多個 VPC 和本地位置之間的連接管理。透過VPC附著和VPN附著，可以輕鬆擴展網路架構，滿足未來的擴展需求。此選項滿足「簡化管理」和「可擴展性」的要求，因此是正確的。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: is a centralized network connectivity solution that simplifies management of connectivity between multiple VPCs and on-premises locations. Through VPC attachments and VPN attachments, the network architecture can be easily expanded to meet future expansion needs. This option meets the requirements of "simplified management" and "scalability" and is therefore correct.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 使用 AWS 的公司需要一個解決方案來預測每月製造流程所需的資源。此解決方案必須使用目前儲存在 Amazon S3 儲存桶中的歷史值。該公司沒有機器學習 (ML) 經驗，希望使用託管服務進行培訓和預測。哪一種步驟組合可以滿足這些要求？ （選兩個。）
question_text.en: A company that uses AWS needs a solution to predict the resources needed for manufacturing processes each month. The solution must use historical values that are currently stored in an Amazon S3 bucket. The company has no machine learning (ML) experience and wants to use a managed service for the training and predictions. Which combination of steps will meet these requirements? (Choose two.)

A.zh: 部署 Amazon SageMaker 模型。建立用於推理的 SageMaker 端點。
A.en: Deploy an Amazon SageMaker model. Create a SageMaker endpoint for inference.

B.zh: 使用 Amazon SageMaker 透過 S3 儲存桶中的歷史資料來訓練模型。
B.en: Use Amazon SageMaker to train a model by using the historical data in the S3 bucket.

C.zh: 使用函數 URL 設定 AWS Lambda 函數，該函數使用 Amazon SageMaker 終端節點根據輸入建立預測。
C.en: Configure an AWS Lambda function with a function URL that uses Amazon SageMaker endpoints to create predictions based on the inputs.

D.zh: 使用函數 URL 設定 AWS Lambda 函數，該函數使用 Amazon Forecast 預測器根據輸入建立預測。
D.en: Configure an AWS Lambda function with a function URL that uses an Amazon Forecast predictor to create a prediction based on the inputs.

E.zh: 使用 S3 儲存桶中的歷史資料訓練 Amazon Forsecast 預測器。
E.en: Train an Amazon Forsecast predictor by using the historical data in the S3 bucket.

A.zh: 錯誤。Amazon SageMaker 是一個功能強大的機器學習平臺，但它要求使用者俱備一定的機器學習知識來建構、訓練和部署模型。問題明確指出該公司沒有機器學習解決方案，因為直接使用SageMaker模型並創建點不符合要求。錯誤原因：不符合「無機器學習軸」的條件。
A.en: Incorrect. Amazon SageMaker is a powerful machine learning platform, but it requires users to have certain machine learning knowledge to build, train, and deploy models. The question clearly states that the company does not have a machine learning solution, because directly using the SageMaker model and creating points does not meet the requirements. Reason for the error: The condition of "no machine learning axis" is not met.

B.zh: 錯誤。使用 Amazon SageMaker 訓練模型需要使用者俱備一定的機器學習知識，包括資料處理、模型選擇和超參數調整。該問題必須使用託管服務，而該公司沒有機器學習工具，因此選項不符合要求。錯誤原因：不符合「無機器學習軸」的條件。
B.en: Incorrect. Using Amazon SageMaker to train models requires users to have certain machine learning knowledge, including data processing, model selection, and hyperparameter tuning. The question must use a hosting service, and the company does not have a machine learning tool, so the options do not meet the requirements. Reason for the error: The condition of "no machine learning axis" is not met.

C.zh: 錯誤。此選項也使用 AWS Lambda 函數呼叫 SageMaker 節點進行測試。雖然Lambda函數可以瘋狂調用，但前提是使用者需要先建立並安裝SageMaker模型，這與問題的「無機器學習軸」不一致。錯誤原因：依賴SageMaker模型，不符合問題要求。
C.en: Incorrect. This option also uses an AWS Lambda function to call SageMaker nodes for testing. Although the Lambda function can be called wildly, the prerequisite is that the user needs to build and install a SageMaker model first, which is inconsistent with the "no machine learning axis" of the question. Reason for the error: Relying on the SageMaker model, which does not meet the requirements of the question.

D.zh: 正確。Amazon Forecast 是專為時間序列測試而設計的託管服務，不需要使用者俱備機器學習技能。可選的 Lambda 函數會呼叫 Amazon Forecast 測試程式來產生符合問題要求的測試。使用託管服務滿足「無機器學習軸」的條件。
D.en: Correct. Amazon Forecast is a managed service designed for time series testing and does not require users to have machine learning skills. The optional Lambda function calls the Amazon Forecast tester to generate a test, which meets the requirements of the question. Using a managed service meets the conditions of "no machine learning axis".

E.zh: 正確。可以直接使用S3中的歷史資料來訓練偵測器，使用者不需要具備機器學習能力。選項符合問題要求，能夠滿足公司的需求。使用託管服務滿足「無機器學習軸」的條件。
E.en: Correct. You can directly use historical data in S3 to train the detector, and the user does not need to have machine learning capabilities. The options match the question requirements and can meet the needs of the company. Using a managed service meets the conditions of "no machine learning axis".

answer_text.zh: D. 使用函數 URL 設定 AWS Lambda 函數，該函數使用 Amazon Forecast 預測器根據輸入建立預測。；E. 使用 S3 儲存桶中的歷史資料訓練 Amazon Forsecast 預測器。
answer_text.en: D. Configure an AWS Lambda function with a function URL that uses an Amazon Forecast predictor to create a prediction based on the inputs.; E. Train an Amazon Forsecast predictor by using the historical data in the S3 bucket.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：DE (46%)； AB (46%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：Amazon Forecast 是專為時間序列測試而設計的託管服務，不需要使用者俱備機器學習技能。可選的 Lambda 函數會呼叫 Amazon Forecast 測試程式來產生符合問題要求的測試。使用託管服務滿足「無機器學習軸」的條件。；可以直接使用S3中的歷史資料來訓練偵測器，使用者不需要具備機器學習能力。選項符合問題要求，能夠滿足公司的需求。使用託管服務滿足「無機器學習軸」的條件。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: DE (46%); AB (46%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Amazon Forecast is a managed service designed for time series testing and does not require users to have machine learning skills. The optional Lambda function calls the Amazon Forecast tester to generate a test, which meets the requirements of the question. Using a managed service meets the conditions of "no machine learning axis".; You can directly use historical data in S3 to train the detector, and the user does not need to have machine learning capabilities. The options match the question requirements and can meet the needs of the company. Using a managed service meets the conditions of "no machine learning axis".

questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司管理 AWS Organizations 中的 AWS 帳戶。為帳戶配置 AWS IAM Identity Center (AWS Single Sign-On) 和 AWS Control Tower。該公司希望管理所有帳戶的多個使用者權限。這些權限將由多個 IAM 使用者使用，並且必須在開發人員和管理員團隊之間分配。每個團隊需要不同的權限。該公司想要一個包含兩個團隊都僱用的新用戶的解決方案。哪種解決方案能夠以最少的營運開銷滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company manages AWS accounts in AWS Organizations. AWS IAM Identity Center (AWS Single Sign-On) and AWS Control Tower are configured for the accounts. The company wants to manage multiple user permissions across all the accounts. The permissions will be used by multiple IAM users and must be split between the developer and administrator teams. Each team requires different permissions. The company wants a solution that includes new users that are hired on both teams. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

A.zh: 在 IAM Identity Center 中為每個帳戶建立單獨的使用者。在 IAM Identity Center 中建立單獨的開發人員和管理員群組。將使用者分配到適當的群組。為每個群組建立自訂 IAM 策略以設定細粒度的權限。
A.en: Create individual users in IAM Identity Center for each account. Create separate developer and administrator groups in IAM Identity Center. Assign the users to the appropriate groups. Create a custom IAM policy for each group to set fine-grained permissions.

B.zh: 在 IAM Identity Center 中為每個帳戶建立單獨的使用者。在 IAM Identity Center 中建立單獨的開發人員和管理員群組。將使用者分配到適當的群組。根據需要將 AWS 託管 IAM 策略附加到每個使用者以獲得細粒度的權限。
B.en: Create individual users in IAM Identity Center for each account. Create separate developer and administrator groups in IAM Identity Center. Assign the users to the appropriate groups. Attach AWS managed IAM policies to each user as needed for fine-grained permissions.

C.zh: 在 IAM Identity Center 中建立個人使用者。在 IAM Identity Center 中建立新的開發人員和管理員群組。建立新的權限集，其中包括每個群組的適當 IAM 策略。將新群組分配給適當的帳戶。將新權限集指派給新群組。當僱用新用戶時，將他們添加到適當的群組中。
C.en: Create individual users in IAM Identity Center. Create new developer and administrator groups in IAM Identity Center. Create new permission sets that include the appropriate IAM policies for each group. Assign the new groups to the appropriate accounts. Assign the new permission sets to the new groups. When new users are hired, add them to the appropriate group.

D.zh: 在 IAM Identity Center 中建立個人使用者。建立新的權限集，其中包括適合每個使用者的 IAM 策略。將使用者分配到適當的帳戶。向特定帳戶內的使用者授予額外的 IAM 權限。當僱用新用戶時，將其新增至 IAM Identity Center 並將其指派給帳戶。
D.en: Create individual users in IAM Identity Center. Create new permission sets that include the appropriate IAM policies for each user. Assign the users to the appropriate accounts. Grant additional IAM permissions to the users from within specific accounts. When new users are hired, add them to IAM Identity Center and assign them to the accounts.

A.zh: 錯誤。建立使用者和群組是正確的，但為每個群組建立自訂 IAM 政策會增加操作開銷，尤其是在需要頻繁更新權限時。錯誤原因：沒有利用權限集的功能，導致管理複雜度增加。
A.en: Incorrect. Creating users and groups is correct, but creating custom IAM policies for each group increases operational overhead, especially when frequent permission updates are required. Reason for the error: The function of permission sets is not utilized, resulting in increased management complexity.

B.zh: 錯誤。建立使用者和群組是正確的，但將 AWS 託管策略直接附加到使用者會導致管理複雜性增加，尤其是隨著使用者數量的增加。錯誤原因：沒有使用權限集（Permission Sets）的功能，直接管理使用者權限不符合最小執行開銷的要求。
B.en: Incorrect. Creating users and groups is correct, but attaching AWS managed policies directly to users results in increased management complexity, especially as the number of users increases. Reason for the error: The function of permission sets (Permission Sets) is not used, and directly managing user permissions does not meet the requirements of minimum operating overhead.

C.zh: 正確。這是利用 IAM Identity Center 的權限集和群組功能來簡化跨帳戶權限管理的最佳實務。權限集可以實現權限的集中管理，而使用群組可以方便的將使用者新增至群組中，無需額外的操作。
C.en: Correct. This is a best practice that leverages the Permission Sets and Groups features of IAM Identity Center to simplify cross-account permission management. Permission sets allow centralized management of permissions, and the use of groups makes it easy to add users to the group without additional operations.

D.zh: 錯誤。建立使用者和權限集是正確的，但為單一使用者建立權限集會增加管理複雜度。錯誤原因：沒有使用群組（Groups）的功能，導致新增使用者時需要額外的權限配置，增加了操作開銷。
D.en: Incorrect. Creating users and permission sets is correct, but creating permission sets for individual users increases management complexity. Reason for the error: The function of groups (Groups) is not used, resulting in additional permission configuration being required when adding new users, which increases operational overhead.

answer_text.zh: C. 在 IAM Identity Center 中建立個人使用者。在 IAM Identity Center 中建立新的開發人員和管理員群組。建立新的權限集，其中包括每個群組的適當 IAM 策略。將新群組分配給適當的帳戶。將新權限集指派給新群組。當僱用新用戶時，將他們添加到適當的群組中。
answer_text.en: C. Create individual users in IAM Identity Center. Create new developer and administrator groups in IAM Identity Center. Create new permission sets that include the appropriate IAM policies for each group. Assign the new groups to the appropriate accounts. Assign the new permission sets to the new groups. When new users are hired, add them to the appropriate group.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：C (100%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：這是利用 IAM Identity Center 的權限集和群組功能來簡化跨帳戶權限管理的最佳實務。權限集可以實現權限的集中管理，而使用群組可以方便的將使用者新增至群組中，無需額外的操作。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: This is a best practice that leverages the Permission Sets and Groups features of IAM Identity Center to simplify cross-account permission management. Permission sets allow centralized management of permissions, and the use of groups makes it easy to add users to the group without additional operations.

questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司希望標準化其 Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) 磁碟區加密策略。該公司還希望最大限度地減少操作卷加密檢查所需的成本和配置工作。哪種解決方案可以滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company wants to standardize its Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) volume encryption strategy. The company also wants to minimize the cost and configuration effort required to operate the volume encryption check. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 編寫 API 呼叫來描述 EBS 磁碟區並確認 EBS 磁碟區已加密。使用 Amazon EventBridge 安排 AWS Lambda 函數來執行 API 呼叫。
A.en: Write API calls to describe the EBS volumes and to confirm the EBS volumes are encrypted. Use Amazon EventBridge to schedule an AWS Lambda function to run the API calls.

B.zh: 編寫 API 呼叫來描述 EBS 磁碟區並確認 EBS 磁碟區已加密。對 AWS Fargate 任務執行 API 呼叫。
B.en: Write API calls to describe the EBS volumes and to confirm the EBS volumes are encrypted. Run the API calls on an AWS Fargate task.

C.zh: 建立需要在 EBS 磁碟區上使用標籤的 AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) 政策。使用 AWS Cost Explorer 顯示未正確標記的資源。手動加密未標記的資源。
C.en: Create an AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) policy that requires the use of tags on EBS volumes. Use AWS Cost Explorer to display resources that are not properly tagged. Encrypt the untagged resources manually.

D.zh: 為 Amazon EBS 建立 AWS Config 規則，以評估磁碟區是否已加密，並在磁碟區未加密時標記該磁碟區。
D.en: Create an AWS Config rule for Amazon EBS to evaluate if a volume is encrypted and to flag the volume if it is not encrypted.

A.zh: 錯誤。雖然可以透過API呼叫檢查EBS磁碟區是否加密，並使用EventBridge調度Lambda函數，但這種方法需要額外的開發工作和維護成本，不符合「最小化成本和配置工作量」的要求。
A.en: Incorrect. Although it is possible to check whether the EBS volume is encrypted through API calls and use EventBridge to schedule Lambda functions, this method requires additional development work and maintenance costs and does not meet the requirement of "minimizing cost and configuration effort".

B.zh: 錯誤。與選項A類似，這種方法也需要額外的開發工作和維護成本，並且還引入了Fargate任務的運行成本，不符合問題的要求。
B.en: Incorrect. Similar to option A, this method also requires additional development work and maintenance costs, and also introduces the running cost of the Fargate task, which does not meet the requirements of the question.

C.zh: 錯誤。此選項不符合問題的要求，因為它依賴於對未標記資源的手動加密，無法自動進行加密檢查，並且無法直接評估EBS磁碟區是否加密。
C.en: Incorrect. This option does not meet the requirements of the question because it relies on manual encryption of unmarked resources, cannot automate encryption checks, and cannot directly evaluate whether the EBS volume is encrypted.

D.zh: 正確。AWS Config 規則可以自動評估 EBS 磁碟區是否已加密並標記未加密磁碟區。此方法不需要額外的開發工作，成本低，完全滿足問題的要求。
D.en: Correct. AWS Config rules can automatically evaluate whether EBS volumes are encrypted and mark unencrypted volumes. This method requires no additional development work, has low cost, and fully meets the requirements of the question.

answer_text.zh: D. 為 Amazon EBS 建立 AWS Config 規則，以評估磁碟區是否已加密，並在磁碟區未加密時標記該磁碟區。
answer_text.en: D. Create an AWS Config rule for Amazon EBS to evaluate if a volume is encrypted and to flag the volume if it is not encrypted.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：D (100%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：AWS Config 規則可以自動評估 EBS 磁碟區是否已加密並標記未加密磁碟區。此方法不需要額外的開發工作，成本低，完全滿足問題的要求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: D (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: AWS Config rules can automatically evaluate whether EBS volumes are encrypted and mark unencrypted volumes. This method requires no additional development work, has low cost, and fully meets the requirements of the question.

questions.domain: 領域 4：設計成本優化架構 (Design Cost-Optimized Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司定期將 GB 大小的檔案上傳到 Amazon S3。該公司上傳文件後，使用一組 Amazon EC2 Spot 執行個體對文件格式進行轉碼。當公司將資料從本機資料中心上傳到 Amazon S3 以及公司將資料從 Amazon S3 下載到 EC2 執行個體時，公司需要擴展吞吐量。哪些解決方案可以滿足這些要求？ （選兩個。）
question_text.en: A company regularly uploads GB-sized files to Amazon S3. After the company uploads the files, the company uses a fleet of Amazon EC2 Spot Instances to transcode the file format. The company needs to scale throughput when the company uploads data from the on-premises data center to Amazon S3 and when the company downloads data from Amazon S3 to the EC2 instances. Which solutions will meet these requirements? (Choose two.)

A.zh: 使用 S3 儲存桶存取點，而不是直接存取 S3 儲存桶。
A.en: Use the S3 bucket access point instead of accessing the S3 bucket directly.

B.zh: 將檔案上傳到多個 S3 儲存桶中。
B.en: Upload the files into multiple S3 buckets.

C.zh: 使用 S3 分段上傳。
C.en: Use S3 multipart uploads.

D.zh: 並行取得物件的多個位元組範圍。
D.en: Fetch multiple byte-ranges of an object in parallel.

E.zh: 上傳檔案時為每個物件加上隨機前綴。
E.en: Add a random prefix to each object when uploading the files.

A.zh: 錯誤。S3 Bucket Access Point主要用於簡化對S3的存取管理，特別是在多帳戶或多應用場景下，但它不能直接提高上傳或下載吞吐量。
A.en: Incorrect. S3 Bucket Access Point is mainly used to simplify access management to S3, especially in multi-account or multi-application scenarios, but it cannot directly improve upload or download throughput.

B.zh: 錯誤。使用多個S3 Bucket無法提高單一Bucket的吞吐量，並且增加了管理複雜度。優化吞吐量的重點是如何有效率地使用單一Bucket。
B.en: Incorrect. Using multiple S3 Buckets cannot improve the throughput of a single Bucket, and increases management complexity. The focus of optimizing throughput is how to use a single Bucket efficiently.

C.zh: 正確。S3 Multipart Upload允許將大檔案分成多個部分並行上傳，從而顯著提高上傳速度，尤其是GB級別的大檔案。
C.en: Correct. S3 Multipart Upload allows large files to be divided into multiple parts and uploaded in parallel, thereby significantly improving upload speed, especially for large files in the GB level.

D.zh: 正確。並行檢索物件的多個位元組範圍可以顯著提高下載速度。這是 S3 效能優化的推薦實踐之一。
D.en: Correct. Parallel retrieval of multiple byte ranges of objects can significantly improve download speeds. This is one of the recommended practices for S3 performance optimization.

E.zh: 錯誤。添加crash前綴主要是為了優化S3的分區效能。然而，在現代S3架構中，分區效能已經被最佳化，crash前綴的作用有限。
E.en: Incorrect. Adding the crash prefix is ​​mainly used to optimize the partition performance of S3. However, in the modern S3 architecture, the partition performance has been optimized, and the crash prefix has limited effect.

answer_text.zh: C. 使用 S3 分段上傳。；D. 並行取得物件的多個位元組範圍。
answer_text.en: C. Use S3 multipart uploads.; D. Fetch multiple byte-ranges of an object in parallel.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：CD (100%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：S3 Multipart Upload允許將大檔案分成多個部分並行上傳，從而顯著提高上傳速度，尤其是GB級別的大檔案。；並行檢索物件的多個位元組範圍可以顯著提高下載速度。這是 S3 效能優化的推薦實踐之一。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: CD (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: S3 Multipart Upload allows large files to be divided into multiple parts and uploaded in parallel, thereby significantly improving upload speed, especially for large files in the GB level.; Parallel retrieval of multiple byte ranges of objects can significantly improve download speeds. This is one of the recommended practices for S3 performance optimization.
